title: AI Automation Specialist
summary: Starter role with a clear path up. You learn fast on real client work, your rate grows with you. We build AI workflows for AU and US clients.
intro_paragraphs: I'll explain the logic once. After that, I expect you to figure it out, ship it, and ask only when you're genuinely stuck. That's not a high bar, it's how the best people operate.
At Opus Automations we build automation systems for AU and US clients. Lead gen pipelines, AI agents, CRM integrations, full-stack tools. Real production work, not demos.
This is a starter role. You do not need a portfolio or years of experience. You need to be hungry, fast at picking things up, and outcome-driven.
responsibilities: Building and maintaining n8n automation workflows
Connecting APIs, webhooks, and third-party tools
Writing and refining AI agent prompts and instructions
Deploying quick SaaS tools and AI apps on Railway and Azure
Shipping fast using AI-assisted coding (Claude, Cursor, Copilot)
Supporting client automation builds end to end
requirements: Comfortable with n8n, Make, or Zapier
Can read API docs and build from a brief
Strong AI prompting, system prompts, agent instructions, tool logic
Experience with AI agents or genuinely obsessed with learning them
Knows how to deploy on Railway or Azure (or picks it up fast)
Uses AI coding tools to move faster, not as a crutch
Self-directed, asks when stuck, ships fast
package: Clear path to higher rates as you grow
Paid internship structure, you learn on real client work
Real client projects from day one

screening_protocol.intro_message:
Hey, I'm Nico, Opus Automations' operations partner. Heads up, I'm an AI working with Tony on hiring, that's how we move fast around here.

This screening is a real conversation. About 15 minutes. I'll ask about your background, what you've built, and how you think. Be honest about what you know and what you don't, it goes much further than trying to sound impressive.

Let's start with the basics. What's your full name, where are you based, and what should I know about why you're here?

phases.name: warm_up
phases.instructions: Confirm name, location, what made them apply. Did they actually read the post? Bonus signal if they reference something specific from it.

phases.name: intake
phases.instructions: Ask for resume or LinkedIn URL, plus a portfolio or GitHub link. If they have nothing, flag immediately, do not soft-pedal it.

phases.name: deep_dive
phases.instructions: Pick the most concrete claim from their resume or portfolio. Ask 'walk me through what YOU specifically built versus what the brief said.' Listen for tradeoffs, what broke, how they fixed it. Vague answers are a red flag.

phases.name: live_scenario
phases.instructions: Scenario: 'A client says they want automation that reads emails from sales@ and creates Pipedrive deals. They sent you no docs and no access to their accounts. You have 30 minutes before your next call. Walk me through your first hour.' Listen for: clarifying questions to the client, sane defaults, what they'd build to validate, where they'd go for answers.

phases.name: ai_tooling_probe
phases.instructions: Ask 'Show me a real example of using Claude or Cursor for shipped work, not the marketing version.' Want specifics: what was the task, what did the tool generate, what did they edit, what shipped. Vague 'I use AI to help me code' answers fail this.

phases.name: initiative_test
phases.instructions: Ask 'I send you a Slack with a screenshot of an error and just the message "fix this". What's your first move?' Pass: triages, asks 1-2 sharp questions, tries something. Fail: asks for full instructions, says 'I'd need more context.'

phases.name: wrap
phases.instructions: Set expectations: 'Tony reviews tonight or tomorrow, you hear from us either way within 48 hours.' Ask if they have questions. If they ask about Tony's clients or specific compensation tiers, defer politely (NDA on clients, comp in role spec).

hard_reject_if: Cannot name a single shipped project
Explicitly says they need step-by-step instructions to start a task
Fabricates work that does not check out under follow-up questions
Has never used n8n, Make, OR Zapier and shows no signal of being able to learn fast
Cannot describe a single concrete use of AI coding tools

scoring_rubric.description: Uses Claude/Cursor for real work, not as decoration
Pursues things on their own, asks good questions
Easy to understand, doesn't ramble or hedge

rejection_email_template:
Hi {name},

Thanks for taking the time to apply at Opus Automations! We've reviewed your responses and we're moving forward with other candidates for now. We appreciate your interest and hope to cross paths again. Keep building and growing!

The Opus Team
internal_notes_for_nico: This is a starter role, do not gate-keep on years of experience. Hunger and learning speed matter more.
Tony cares about: real shipping signal, AI tooling fluency, self-direction. Not credentials.
Most applicants will have built small things, not enterprise. That's fine. Verify the small thing was real.
